item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wouldn't get anything from this skip at all had I not read the author's explanation above.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was the first SCP article I've read that I've genuinely not enjoyed. It's just so odd and disjointed (ha). There's nothing attention grabbing or interesting about the object, it's… a floating head that makes things. You mentioned that you coldposted this article, and it's really easy to tell. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why did they a pen to saturn WTF

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, a neat idea I hadn't considered.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
B) the spell starts killing all the other Beniches in the multiverse

Hmm, I would like to see the baseline universe's Benich been taken out, and her head joins all her counterparts. Currently, the final part feels too disjointed from the rest of the article (unless the SCP also has tumours on them as a constant feature).

For some reason, I see this as some sort of multiversal constant seen in every reality where humanity exist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No, it sounds like you've got it. There's a story here, but it's not meant to be super-deep.

+ The way I see it...
– hide block

In another universe, Foundation Magus Benich tried to cure his thyroid cancer himself. Whatever he did, it went super-wrong and not only blew his damn fool head off, but shot it into another universe.

And then, either A) this pattern continues with each Benich in the multiverse, or B) the spell starts killing all the other Beniches in the multiverse, with our Benich being saved because she lives in, y'know, a place they use medicine and not super-destructive, reality-bending magic to solve cancer. There's probably a C, but I forgot it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think I'm missing something here.

The article is constructed well enough - it's technically decent, the parts are all laid out and appear to come to a revelatory point where it's revealed Doctor Benich has a tumor in her throat, but then it just… ends. I don't feel any satisfaction, any resolution or even any sense of absence or emptiness. I just don't understand this. It feels to me like there is an underlying story here - the fact that each of the alterations results in a head, the fact that most of the items are related to Benich and reek of some sort of multiversal fuckup - but I don't see the thread that's supposed to connect them all. Why is it in geosynchronous orbit? Why is it just her head? Why was there a boar in there? etc.

+0 novoting because i think there's something cool here but i'm not smart enough to divine what it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, I'm glad you noticed! :D It just so happens that 'our' universe is the only one where Researcher Benich is female!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The irony is I already fixed a typo in that line…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this but the description says it's the researchers severed head, your first log entry says its the baseline and is male, but your final addendum says the researcher is female. Am I missing something or this is an error?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No non-human instance of SCP-3083-1

Should be SCP-3083?

Otherwise, great work, and sufficiently weird to merit an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Coldpost because I've felt like coldposting recently. Conception to post took 84 hours, I literally came up with this while waking up this morning. Hopefully it doesn't suck too much. (EDIT: I can't math lol)
